meta.title: END-IT — functional outcome after convulsive status epilepticus
meta.authors: Gao Q, Ou-Yang T-P, Sun X-L, Yang F, Wu C, Kang T, Kang X-G, Jiang W
meta.outcome: Risk of an unfavorable functional outcome (modified Rankin Scale 3–6, i.e. death or dependence) at 3 months after discharge, in adults with convulsive status epilepticus.
meta.source_note: END-IT: Gao et al., Crit Care 2016 (doi 10.1186/s13054-016-1221-9, PMID 26916702). Point table from Table 4; per-score probability read from Figure 2; operating characteristics (cut-off ≥3) from Table 5. Externally validated (e.g. Reindl 2018, Bögli 2023, Kanmaz 2025) with more modest discrimination (AUC ~0.65–0.74) in NCSE/neuro-ICU and paediatric cohorts.
meta.cff_info:
What this is. END-IT predicts a poor functional outcome — modified Rankin Scale (mRS) 3–6, i.e. moderate-to-severe disability or death — at 3 months after an episode of convulsive status epilepticus (CSE). It complements survival scores (STESS, EMSE) by focusing on disability among survivors.

How it is scored. Five items are added — Encephalitis, NCSE (non-convulsive/subtle SE), Diazepam resistance, Imaging abnormality (unilateral 1, bilateral/diffuse oedema 2), and Tracheal intubation — for a total of 0–6. The predicted probability of a poor outcome rises steadily with the score.

How well it works. In the derivation cohort (132 patients) discrimination was good (AUC 0.83) and the optimal cut-off was ≥3 (sensitivity 84%, specificity 69%, PPV 70%, NPV 83%), outperforming STESS and EMSE-EACE for functional outcome. External validations show more modest performance (AUC ~0.65–0.74), so treat it as a risk indicator, not a certainty.

Scope. Adults with convulsive SE (median age 25; cerebral anoxia excluded). Per-score probabilities are read from the paper's Figure 2 and are study-population specific. Population-level estimate — always apply clinical judgement.

source_link.label: PubMed
source_link.title: Gao et al., Crit Care 2016 — END-IT

predictors.name: Encephalitis
predictors.assess: Encephalitis as the cause of the status epilepticus (the commonest cause in the derivation cohort). Present = 1 point.

predictors.name: NCSE (non-convulsive / subtle SE)
predictors.assess: Convulsive SE complicated by / evolving into non-convulsive (subtle) status epilepticus — subtle clinical signs with EEG discharges. Present = 1 point.

predictors.name: Diazepam resistance
predictors.assess: Status epilepticus that did not respond to first-line benzodiazepine (diazepam). Present = 1 point.

predictors.name: Neuroimaging abnormality
predictors.assess: Worst brain-imaging finding. No responsible lesion = 0; a unilateral lesion = 1; bilateral lesions or diffuse cerebral oedema = 2.

options.label: Bilateral lesions or diffuse cerebral oedema

predictors.name: Tracheal intubation
predictors.assess: Need for tracheal intubation during the SE episode. Present = 1 point.

model.panel_title: END-IT — poor functional outcome after convulsive SE

rows.label: END-IT 0 — favorable: ~3% risk of poor outcome (below cut-off; NPV 83% at ≥3)
END-IT 1 — favorable: ~9% risk of poor outcome (below cut-off)
END-IT 2 — favorable: ~24% risk of poor outcome (below cut-off)
END-IT 3 — at/above cut-off (≥3): ~49% risk of poor outcome
END-IT 4 — unfavorable: ~72% risk of poor outcome
END-IT 5 — unfavorable: ~88% risk of poor outcome
END-IT 6 — unfavorable: ~96% risk of poor outcome

model.note: Predicted probability of an unfavorable functional outcome (mRS 3–6) at 3 months, read from the END-IT logistic curve (Gao 2016, Fig 2); points from Table 4. The validated decision cut-off is ≥3 (sensitivity 84% / specificity 69% / PPV 70% / NPV 83%; AUC 0.83 in derivation, ~0.65–0.74 externally). Study-population-specific — a risk indicator, not an exact probability.
model.viz_info: Encephalitis, NCSE, diazepam resistance, imaging and tracheal intubation each add points (waterfall). The total (0–6) maps to the predicted risk of a poor functional outcome at 3 months (donut). A score of ≥3 is the validated threshold for a likely poor outcome.

provenance.source: Point allocation taken verbatim from Gao et al., Crit Care 2016, Table 4 (Encephalitis 1, NCSE 1, Diazepam resistance 1, Imaging unilateral 1 / bilateral-or-diffuse-oedema 2, Tracheal intubation 1). Per-score probabilities read from Figure 2 (predicted-probability logistic curve): 0→~3%, 1→~9%, 2→~24%, 3→~49%, 4→~72%, 5→~88%, 6→~96% (consistent with logit ≈ −3.48 + 1.15·score). Operating characteristics (cut-off ≥3) from Table 5. PDF in 0_inbox/papers/status_epilepticus/.
provenance.notes: Score kind, 5 predictors (Imaging 0/1/2, others 0/1), total 0–6. Points are exact (Table 4). Per-score pct digitized from Fig 2 (population-specific predicted probability), so approximate (±~5pp) — flagged in note/cff_info; the validated dichotomy is ≥3. Fills the site's functional-outcome gap (STESS/EMSE/ACD are mortality/survival).
